summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened with a heartfelt message of love and encouragement for the Impact Church family, acknowledging the deep struggles many face, including addiction, illness, loss, betrayal, and shame. He posed the central question of the sermon: 'What are you going to do about it?' He emphasized that while God is our help, we also have a role to play in our spiritual battles. Pastor Travis shared a personal story from his youth about learning to 'suck it up' and persevere through pain, framing it as a spiritual principle of depending on God's strength in our weakness. The sermon provided three clear responses to life's difficulties. First, we must leave the past in the past, adopting a 'next play' mentality and straining toward what is ahead, as instructed in Philippians 3. Second, we must keep pressing on and never look back, using the sobering example of Lot's wife, who became a pillar of salt for longing for her old life. Third, we are to make our misery our ministry, using our pain and trials as a testimony to comfort others and as a weapon against the enemy, just as David used Goliath's own sword. The message concluded with a powerful call to crucify our old sinful nature, live alive in Christ, and use every attack from the enemy as a trophy of God's triumph.
mainPoints: Leave the past in the past by adopting a 'next play' mentality and straining toward what God has ahead.
Keep pressing on and never look back, obeying God's clear instructions to avoid the consequences of longing for an old life.
Make your misery your ministry by using your pain as a testimony to comfort others and as a weapon against the enemy.

versesMentioned.text: Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.
Forget the former things; do not dwell on the past. See, I am doing a new thing! Now it springs up; do you not perceive it? I am making a way in the wilderness and streams in the wasteland.
As soon as they had brought them out, one of them said, 'Flee for your lives! Don't look back, and don't stop anywhere in the plain! Flee to the mountains or you will be swept away!'
But Lot's wife looked back, and she became a pillar of salt.
Remember Lot's wife! Whoever tries to keep their life will lose it, and whoever loses their life will preserve it.
I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me. The life I now live in the body, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself for me.
We are hard pressed on every side, but not crushed; perplexed, but not in despair; persecuted, but not abandoned; struck down, but not destroyed.
who comforts us in all our troubles, so that we can comfort those in any trouble with the comfort we ourselves receive from God.
David ran and stood over him. He took hold of the Philistine's sword and drew it from the sheath. After he killed him, he cut off his head with the sword.

Monday.scripture: Philippians 3:13-14 (NIV) - '...Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead...'
Monday.action: Identify one thing from your past you are dwelling on. Write it down, pray for God's help to release it, and then physically discard the paper as an act of faith.
Monday.prayer: God, help me to forget what is behind. Give me the strength to strain forward toward the future you have for me, trusting in your grace.

Tuesday.scripture: Genesis 19:26 (NIV) - 'But Lot's wife looked back, and she became a pillar of salt.'
Tuesday.action: When a tempting thought about an old habit or sinful pattern arises today, immediately say 'next play' out loud and pray for strength to keep moving forward.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, guard my heart from longing for my old life. Help me to obey your clear instructions and fix my spiritual eyes on the freedom you provide.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 4:8-9 (NIV) - 'We are hard pressed on every side, but not crushed...'
Wednesday.action: Reflect on a current challenge. Write down how you feel 'hard pressed' and then next to it, write 'but not crushed,' acknowledging God's sustaining power.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, thank you that in my pressures and perplexities, I am not crushed or in despair. Your power holds me up today.

Thursday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 1:4 (NIV) - '...so that we can comfort those in any trouble with the comfort we ourselves receive from God.'
Thursday.action: Reach out to one person today—via text, call, or in person—and share a brief word of encouragement based on a struggle God has brought you through.
Thursday.prayer: God, use my past pain for your purpose. Show me someone I can comfort today with the same comfort you have given me.

Friday.scripture: Galatians 2:20 (NIV) - 'I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me.'
Friday.action: Identify one 'fleshly desire' you need to crucify. Spend time in prayer, consciously surrendering that area of your life to Christ's control.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, I choose to crucify my old nature today. I am a dead man walking to myself, but alive in you. Live through me.

Saturday.scripture: 1 Samuel 17:51 (NIV) - '...he cut off his head with the sword.'
Saturday.action: Name a 'giant' in your life (fear, addiction, shame). Write a declaration of faith, stating that with God's help, you will stand over this enemy in victory.
Saturday.prayer: Mighty God, you are my champion. I trust you to give me victory over my giants. I will use the enemy's attacks as a trophy of your power.
